verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is a secreted serine protease of the kallikrein family. It is synthesized with a signal peptide and prepro-peptide, processed through the secretory pathway, and released into the extracellular milieu — it has no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no outer-leaflet lipidation. Its canonical role is as a secreted enzyme in prostatic fluid/serum. Pre-checklist: (1) No cell-surface-directed antibody/ADC clinical program targets the membrane-anchored form — serum KLK2 is a diagnostic biomarker, not a surface therapeutic target. (2) No TM precursor sheddase biology — it is constitutively secreted, not shed from a TM anchor. (3) No documented wash-resistant TM-partner tethering. (4) No documented PM minority pool. (5) No gene-specific evidence of cancer-state ecto-display, cell-death-induced surface exposure, germline-restricted surface display, or activation-induced PM translocation. It is secreted_only.
